static_check_catalog.description: Test function contains no pytest assertions.
Assertion is always true, such as assert True or assert X or True.
Only assertion checks returncode == 0.
Assertion allows non-zero exits with returncode in a list.
Test body is just pass.
Assertion uses A or B instead of asserting an exact outcome.
If branch asserts with no else, silently passing when false.
Both if and else branches assert, suggesting non-deterministic behavior.
Except handler with pass swallows failures.
All assertions only check returncode, len, or isdigit.
Substring check is shorter than 15 characters.
Test writes to its own golden file.
Golden file is referenced in docstring but never compared with ==.
Golden file is mentioned in docstring but not found in the test body.
Loop-only assertions have no pre-loop length check.
Sole assertion is len(x) > N.
Assertion uses len(x) >= N, a relative bound that verifies no content.
any()/all() assertion has no non-empty guard.
path.exists() assertion has no content assertion.
All assertions are negative checks such as not in or !=.
Missing or too-short CATCHES docstring.
